packageText:
# Subacute Subdural Hematoma

Canonical hierarchy: All Categories > Basic > Neuroradiology > Brain > Primary Effects of CNS Trauma > Subacute Subdural Hematoma
Canonical deck path: Corebook::Neuro::Brain::Primary Effects of CNS Trauma::Subacute Subdural Hematoma
Primary source posture: RadPrimer supplies the canonical hierarchy/backbone; STATdx supplies supplemental depth and the default selected image set where it visually replaces RadPrimer duplicates.
Core evidence: no auditable Core Radiology source was supplied in this bundle, so Core-specific support is not asserted.

## Source Coverage And Image Gate

STATdx covers the RadPrimer image teaching set, but the coverage is not uniformly exact. Actual `image_evidence/` files were inspected before classification. RadPrimer images 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 10 have visually exact same-screenshot or same-graphic STATdx counterparts and are archive-optional. RadPrimer image 9 has related STATdx membrane-enhancement coverage but is a different axial MR image, so it remains selected as recognition reinforcement.

RadPrimer image 1 -> STATdx image 1: exact duplicate mechanism graphic; use STATdx image 1 as the selected primary.
RadPrimer image 2 -> STATdx image 2: exact duplicate density-evolution graph; use STATdx image 2.
RadPrimer image 3 -> STATdx image 24: exact duplicate axial NECT right isodense sSDH; use STATdx image 24. STATdx images 3 and 4 remain selected as alternate axial/coronal examples, not duplicates.
RadPrimer image 4 -> STATdx image 25: exact duplicate bilateral balanced isodense sSDH; use STATdx image 25. STATdx images 9 and 10 remain selected as the intracranial-hypotension bilateral cluster.
RadPrimer image 5 -> STATdx image 5: exact duplicate late sSDH with cortical atrophy; use STATdx image 5.
RadPrimer image 6 -> STATdx image 26: exact duplicate different-age bilateral SDHs; use STATdx image 26.
RadPrimer image 7 -> STATdx image 6: exact duplicate axial T1 MR late sSDH; use STATdx image 6.
RadPrimer image 8 -> STATdx image 7: exact duplicate axial FLAIR MR late sSDH; use STATdx image 7.
RadPrimer image 9 -> STATdx image 15: near duplicate/conceptual reinforcement for enhancing outer membrane; keep both RadPrimer image 9 and STATdx image 15 selected because the visual evidence shows different axial MR images.
RadPrimer image 10 -> STATdx image 8: exact duplicate coronal T1 C+ MR membrane image; use STATdx image 8.

## Fused Teaching Backbone

### Definition And Time Window

- [Both] Subacute subdural hematoma is a subdural collection about 3 days to 3 weeks old, located between arachnoid and dura or within the inner dural border cell layer.
- [Both] The collection contains partially liquefied clot and resorbing blood products surrounded by granulation tissue membrane.
- [Both] Trauma may be minor, especially in older adults, because bridging cortical veins are stretched or torn as they cross the subdural space.
- [Both] The hematoma can enlarge through repetitive hemorrhage from the outer membrane; membrane enhancement marks an unstable collection prone to rehemorrhage.

### CT Recognition Pattern

- [Both] On NECT, subacute SDH is crescentic, iso- to hypodense, extraaxial, and can spread diffusely over a hemisphere.
- [Both] It may be the same density as cortex. The search pattern is displaced gray-white interface, compressed sulci, and inwardly displaced dots of CSF rather than relying only on obvious hyperdensity.
- [Both] SDH may cross sutures but not dural attachments.
- [Both] Density evolves from acute hyperdensity toward isodensity at about 7-10 days and hypodensity around 10-14 days; recurrent hemorrhage can create mixed density.
- [STATdx] STATdx images 3 and 4 add alternate axial and coronal right-sided examples of difficult isodense sSDH detection.
- [STATdx] STATdx images 9 and 10 form an atomic intracranial-hypotension cluster: bilateral nearly isodense SDHs plus sagittal CT signs of brain sagging and a plump pituitary.

### MR Recognition Pattern

- [Both] T1 signal is iso- to hyperintense; T2 is often hyperintense; FLAIR does not suppress like CSF.
- [Both] DWI signal varies with hematoma age and may show a double-layer appearance; a hyperintense band can indicate relatively fresh bleeding from the outer membrane.
- [STATdx] Add SWI/GRE to trauma/hemorrhage MR protocols; the source package specifically broadens the protocol advice from GRE to SWI/GRE.
- [Both] T1 C+ may show enhancing membranes. Outer membrane enhancement supports late/unstable sSDH and helps distinguish sSDH or chronic SDH from pachymeningeal mimics.
- [Both] MR is more sensitive than CT for SDH and for additional traumatic brain injuries.
- [STATdx] STATdx images 11-15 provide an atomic multimodality cluster demonstrating CT, T1, FLAIR, T2, and T1 C+ behavior of subacute collections.
- [STATdx] STATdx images 16 and 17 add early/fluid-level and DWI examples, including the caution that blood signal on DWI is not always predictable.

### Differential Diagnosis

- [Both] Other subdural collections include effusion, hygroma, and empyema.
- [Both] Pachymeningopathies can mimic thickened dura; actively search for intracranial hypotension signs when trauma history is absent.
- [Both] Chronic dural sinus thrombosis remains in the differential for subdural-type collections and dural/pachymeningeal findings.
- [Both] Signal on MR often parallels intracerebral hematoma evolution, but exceptions occur, especially in early subacute hematomas.

### Pathology And Clinical Context

- [Both] Etiology centers on bridging vein injury; the source packages also describe a delayed hypersensitivity component with antigen release during clot liquefaction and antigen-presenting cells in the neomembrane.
- [Both] Gross pathology is granulation tissue with resorbing blood products. The outer membrane is tied to enlargement and repetitive hemorrhage; the inner membrane is tied to liquefaction of SDH.
- [Both] Presentation can be subtle and delayed, including headache, seizure, or focal neurologic deficit.
- [Both] Demographics include young patients and older adults, with male predominance in the source article.
- [STATdx] STATdx image 18 adds an infant nonaccidental-trauma scenario, preserving the pediatric/abuse context as a distinct recognition example.

### Diagnostic Checklist

- [Both] Initial screen is NECT; consider contrast-enhanced CT for membranes or loculations.
- [Both] On MR, include FLAIR, DWI, and SWI/GRE for hemorrhage or trauma indications.
- [Both] If sSDH appears without a trauma history, evaluate coagulation status and search for intracranial hypotension: brain sagging, dura-arachnoid thickening, engorged dural venous sinuses, and plump pituitary.
- [Both] Report mass effect, sulcal compression, midline shift/herniation if present, bilateral balance, mixed density suggesting rebleeding, and membrane enhancement.

## Selected Image Atlas For Future Generation

Use the following images by default for download and teaching. Exact RadPrimer duplicates are retained in the registry for audit traceability, while RadPrimer image 9 remains selected.

- STATdx image 1: mechanism graphic with bridging vein, membranes, mass effect, and the 3-day to 3-week age window.
- STATdx image 2: density evolution curve; pair with CT images when teaching why sSDH becomes hard to see.
- STATdx images 3, 4, and 24: right isodense sSDH recognition across axial and coronal CT; STATdx image 24 is the exact counterpart for RadPrimer image 3, while STATdx images 3 and 4 are reinforcement.
- STATdx images 5 and 26: late/atrophy and different-age bilateral SDH examples, replacing RadPrimer images 5 and 6.
- STATdx images 6, 7, 8 plus RadPrimer image 9: atomic late-sSDH MR cluster across T1, FLAIR, axial T1 C+ FS membrane enhancement, and coronal T1 C+ membrane anatomy.
- STATdx images 9 and 10: atomic bilateral isodense SDH/intracranial hypotension cluster.
- STATdx images 11-15: atomic multimodality subdural collection cluster; CT, T1, FLAIR, T2, and contrast-enhanced membrane behavior.
- STATdx images 16 and 17: early/fluid-level and DWI appearances, including unstable membrane-risk language.
- STATdx image 18: infant suspected nonaccidental trauma with bilateral SDH after burr hole; keep pediatric/procedure context intact.
- STATdx image 19: mixed-density sSDH with internal membranes and recent hemorrhage into a preexisting collection.
- STATdx image 20: septated nonacute bilateral subdurals and the FLAIR chronicity caveat.
- STATdx images 21-23: atomic differing-age/methemoglobin MR cluster.
- STATdx image 25: bilateral balanced isodense sSDHs, replacing RadPrimer image 4.

## Downstream Use Notes

- Use `selectedPrimaryImageIds` for default image download and teaching.
- Use `archiveOptionalImageIds` only for exact duplicate audit recovery; do not download them by default.
- Preserve same-patient, follow-up, procedure, and multimodality clusters as atomic unless a later source explicitly documents a safe split.
- Keep human-facing prose labels clean, such as RadPrimer image 9 or STATdx image 4. Use short IDs such as RP-09 and SDX-04 only in registry, filenames, and traceability fields.

sourceAttributionRules: Use [RadPrimer], [STATdx], or [Both] in derived content when attribution matters.
Use clean human-facing labels such as RadPrimer image 9 and STATdx image 4 in prose.
Keep RP-09 and SDX-04 style IDs in registry, filenames, manifest, and traceability fields.

sourceSelectionPlan.imageCurationPolicy: Exact duplicates may be archived; near duplicates and conceptual replacements remain selected as recognition reinforcement unless visually identical or unusable. Download only selectedPrimaryImageIds by default.
sourceSelectionPlan.duplicateEvidencePolicy: Exact duplicate decisions in this package are based on actual image_evidence file inspection showing the same slice/screenshot/graphic. Caption-only or topic-only similarity was not used for exact duplicate status.

generatorInstructions: Do not generate cards or a lecture from this handoff alone.
Use packageText as the source-qualified master package.
Use selectedPrimaryImageIds for default image download and teaching. ArchiveOptionalImageIds are retained for audit/completeness only.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic shows a typical subacute subdural hematoma (sSDH) . Inset shows the traversing "bridging" vein and developing membranes . These are often related to relatively minor trauma in the elderly.
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: SDHs decrease by ~ 1.5 HU/day. By 7-10 days, blood in the hematoma becomes isodense with the cortex. By ~ 10-14 days, it is hypodense compared to the adjacent brain.
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT scan shows a right sSDH that is isodense with the underlying cortex. Note the inward displacement of "dots" of CSF in compressed, underlying sulci. The gray matter-white matter (GM-WM) interface is "buckled" inwards (compared with normal-appearing left side).
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Bilateral "balanced" isodense sSDHs are present in the case. Note that both GM-WM interfaces are inwardly displaced and the underlying sulci are compressed. A displaced "dot" of CSF is seen under the left sSDH.
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT scan in an elderly patient with a late sSDH and moderate cortical atrophy shows the difference between the nearly isodense SDH and the hypodense CSF in the underlying compressed subarachnoid spaces .
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: SDHs of different ages are common in elderly anticoagulated patients. The right SDH is subacute (isodense with underlying brain), and the left-sided collection is more chronic-appearing. Note the severe compression of the underlying brain and sulci. The falx is partially calcified.
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1WI MR in a 61-year-old man shows a late subacute SDH that appears nearly isointense with cortex and only slightly more hyperintense than the adjacent sulcal CSF. Note the thin hyperintense membrane overlying the sSDH .
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in the same patient shows the sSDH as uniformly hyperintense . Note mild compression and effacement of the underlying sulci compared to the normal left hemispheric sulci.
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ FS MR in the same patient shows the outer membrane is thick and enhances uniformly. The sSDH is slightly hyperintense compared to sulcal CSF.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: unique RadPrimer axial T1 C+ FS membrane-enhancement view without a visually exact STATdx counterpart
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Same membrane-enhancement teaching concept as STATdx image 15, but visual inspection shows a different axial MR image, so it remains selected for recognition reinforcement.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T1 C+ MR obtained slightly later in the same patient shows the outer membrane surrounding the sSDH is thicker than the thin inner membrane .
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection shows the same slice/screenshot/graphic in the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is the selected primary image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic shows a typical subacute subdural hematoma (sSDH) with local mass effect. Inset shows the traversing "bridging" vein and developing membranes . These are often related to relatively minor trauma in older adults. sSDHs are ~ 3 days to 3 weeks in age.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: mechanism graphic with membranes, bridging vein, local mass effect, and age window
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: SDHs decrease by ~ 1.5 HU/day. By 7-10 days, blood in the hematoma becomes isodense with the cortex. By ~ 10-14 days, it is hypodense compared to the adjacent brain.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: density evolution curve from acute hyperdense to isodense and chronic hypodense SDH
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in this patient with subacute right SDH , which can be difficult to delineate, as it is almost exactly the same attenuation as the underlying gray matter, is shown. The displaced gray-white matter interface toward midline helps make the accurate diagnosis.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial NECT isodense right sSDH recognition by displaced gray-white interface
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal NECT in an older adult with multiple falls shows a subacute right SDH that is isodense with the underlying cortex. Note the inward displacement of CSF and compressed, underlying sulci.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: coronal NECT alternate right sSDH view with compressed sulci and displaced CSF
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT scan in an older adult with a late sSDH and moderate cortical atrophy shows the difference between the nearly isodense SDH and the hypodense CSF in the underlying compressed subarachnoid spaces .
imageRegistry.teachingRole: late sSDH in atrophy showing isodense hematoma versus hypodense compressed CSF
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR in a 61-year-old man shows a late sSDH that appears nearly isointense with the cortex and only slightly more hyperintense than the adjacent sulcal CSF. Note the thin hyperintense membrane overlying the sSDH.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial T1 MR late sSDH nearly isointense with cortex and thin membrane
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR MR in the same patient shows the sSDH as uniformly hyperintense . Note mild compression and effacement of the underlying sulci compared to the normal left hemispheric sulci.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial FLAIR MR same-patient hyperintense sSDH and sulcal effacement
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T1 C+ MR obtained slightly later in the same patient shows the outer membrane surrounding the sSDH is thicker than the thin inner membrane . The sSDH is slightly hyperintense compared to sulcal CSF. DWI may be helpful to exclude an empyema.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: coronal T1 C+ MR same-patient outer and inner membrane anatomy
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal NECT in a patient without a history of trauma demonstrates almost perfectly isodense bilateral SDHs . Look for displaced cortical veins and gray-white matter interface to aid in diagnosis.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: coronal NECT bilateral isodense SDH with displaced cortical veins
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal NECT in the same patient shows findings of intracranial hypotension as the etiology for the bilateral sSDH. There is effacement of the suprasellar cistern, midbrain slumping with a decreased distance, and a "fat" pituitary gland .
imageRegistry.teachingRole: sagittal NECT same-patient intracranial hypotension signs
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT scan in a 59-year-old man with seizures and no definite history of head trauma shows a right frontal extraaxial fluid collection that is slightly hyperdense relative to CSF in the underlying sulci .
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial NECT extraaxial fluid collection slightly denser than CSF
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: T1 MR in the same patient shows there are bilateral subdural collections that appear slightly hyperintense compared with CSF. This is a typical MR finding in sSDHs.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: T1 MR same-patient bilateral subdural collections slightly hyperintense to CSF
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial FLAIR scan in the same patient shows that the subacute fluid collections do not suppress, illustrating their difference compared with normal CSF.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: FLAIR same-patient non-suppression of subacute collections
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: T2 MR in the same patient shows that both collections are isointense with CSF in the underlying subarachnoid cisterns.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: T2 same-patient collections isointense with CSF
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ MR in the same patient shows that the outer membrane of the sSDH enhances . These findings are consistent with late sSDHs.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: T1 C+ same-patient outer membrane enhancement, a near duplicate of the RP membrane teaching point
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Different axial T1 C+ image from RadPrimer image 9; selected as reinforcement rather than archived.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR demonstrates a fluid level within this early sSDH , largely hypointense in signal characteristics. FLAIR MR is generally the most sensitive sequence to identify a small SDH.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: T2 early sSDH fluid level and small-SDH FLAIR sensitivity teaching
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial DWI MR shows mixed signal within the sSDH with a hypointense rim . Signal characteristics of blood are not easily predictable on DWI. Susceptibility is often present, but the significance is uncertain. If a hyperintense outer rim is seen on DWI, it is correlated to "unstable" membranes and predictive of future SDH growth.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: DWI mixed-signal sSDH, hypointense rim, and unstable outer membrane risk concept
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: An infant with suspected nonaccidental trauma shows bilateral SDH , status post recent left-sided burr hole with air. Note that vessels in the subarachnoid space are displaced inward due to mass effect from this sSDH. This SDH is better evaluated on subsequent MR.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: infant suspected nonaccidental trauma with bilateral SDH after burr hole and inwardly displaced vessels
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT shows a mixed-density sSDH . The more linear areas of high attenuation suggest internal membranes with recent hemorrhage into a preexisting collection.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: mixed-density sSDH with internal membranes and recent hemorrhage into a collection
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal FLAIR MR in an 89-year-old man imaged for cognitive decline shows bilateral hypointense subdurals . Septations seen here suggest a nonacute nature with the propensity for recurrent bleeding. Note that FLAIR is inaccurate for staging chronicity of hematomas.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: coronal FLAIR septated nonacute bilateral subdurals and FLAIR chronicity caveat
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T1 MR shows bilateral SDHs of differing ages: An older right hematoma with methemoglobin and an acute-early subacute left SDH .
imageRegistry.teachingRole: coronal T1 MR bilateral SDHs of differing ages with methemoglobin
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal T1 MR shows a hyperintense extraaxial collection compressing adjacent brain parenchyma, consistent with subacute blood (methemoglobin).
imageRegistry.teachingRole: sagittal T1 MR hyperintense extraaxial subacute blood compressing brain
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 MR in the same patient shows the hyperintense left-sided sSDH . Smaller, more recent isointense SDHs are seen over the right hemisphere.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial T1 MR same-patient left sSDH with smaller right isointense SDHs
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Distinct STATdx supplemental image retained for modality, differential, mechanism, age, or scenario breadth.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT shows a right sSDH that is isodense with the underlying cortex. Note the inward displacement of "dots" of CSF in compressed, underlying sulci. The gray matter-white matter interface is "buckled" inward (compared with normal-appearing left side).
imageRegistry.teachingRole: axial NECT right isodense sSDH exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 3
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: Bilateral "balanced" isodense sSDHs are present in the case. Note that both gray matter-white matter interfaces are inwardly displaced, and the underlying sulci are compressed. A displaced "dot" of CSF is seen under the left sSDH.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: bilateral balanced isodense sSDH exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 4
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

imageRegistry.caption: SDHs of different ages are common in older anticoagulated patients. The right SDH is subacute (isodense with underlying brain), and the left-sided collection is more chronic-appearing. Note the severe compression of the underlying brain and sulci. The falx is partially calcified.
imageRegistry.teachingRole: different-age bilateral SDHs exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 6
imageRegistry.classificationRationale: Selected STATdx primary image; actual evidence visually matches the archived RadPrimer counterpart while preserving the STATdx supplemental caption/source depth.

caseClusterGuardrails.rule: Preserve the MR sequence cluster; only exact duplicate source copies are archived.
Keep coronal bilateral SDH and sagittal hypotension signs together.
Keep the same-patient CT/MR sequence together; SDX-15 is not an exact duplicate of RP-09.
Keep the coronal, sagittal, and axial MR views together.

imageDownloadPlan.teachingRole: mechanism graphic with membranes, bridging vein, local mass effect, and age window
density evolution curve from acute hyperdense to isodense and chronic hypodense SDH
axial NECT isodense right sSDH recognition by displaced gray-white interface
coronal NECT alternate right sSDH view with compressed sulci and displaced CSF
late sSDH in atrophy showing isodense hematoma versus hypodense compressed CSF
axial T1 MR late sSDH nearly isointense with cortex and thin membrane
axial FLAIR MR same-patient hyperintense sSDH and sulcal effacement
coronal T1 C+ MR same-patient outer and inner membrane anatomy
coronal NECT bilateral isodense SDH with displaced cortical veins
sagittal NECT same-patient intracranial hypotension signs
axial NECT extraaxial fluid collection slightly denser than CSF
T1 MR same-patient bilateral subdural collections slightly hyperintense to CSF
FLAIR same-patient non-suppression of subacute collections
T2 same-patient collections isointense with CSF
T1 C+ same-patient outer membrane enhancement, a near duplicate of the RP membrane teaching point
T2 early sSDH fluid level and small-SDH FLAIR sensitivity teaching
DWI mixed-signal sSDH, hypointense rim, and unstable outer membrane risk concept
infant suspected nonaccidental trauma with bilateral SDH after burr hole and inwardly displaced vessels
mixed-density sSDH with internal membranes and recent hemorrhage into a collection
coronal FLAIR septated nonacute bilateral subdurals and FLAIR chronicity caveat
coronal T1 MR bilateral SDHs of differing ages with methemoglobin
sagittal T1 MR hyperintense extraaxial subacute blood compressing brain
axial T1 MR same-patient left sSDH with smaller right isointense SDHs
axial NECT right isodense sSDH exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 3
bilateral balanced isodense sSDH exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 4
different-age bilateral SDHs exact visual replacement for RadPrimer image 6
unique RadPrimer axial T1 C+ FS membrane-enhancement view without a visually exact STATdx counterpart

generatorInstructions: Do not generate cards or a lecture from this handoff alone.
Use packageText as the source-qualified master package.
Use selectedPrimaryImageIds for default image download and teaching. ArchiveOptionalImageIds are retained for audit/completeness only.
